speaker: ANNE
text: Set down, set down your honorable load,

speaker: ANNE
text: If honor may be shrouded in a hearse,

speaker: ANNE
text: Whilst I awhile obsequiously lament

speaker: ANNE
text: Th’ untimely fall of virtuous Lancaster.

speaker: ANNE
text: Poor key-cold figure of a holy king,

speaker: ANNE
text: Pale ashes of the house of Lancaster,

speaker: ANNE
text: Thou bloodless remnant of that royal blood,

speaker: ANNE
text: Be it lawful that I invocate thy ghost

speaker: ANNE
text: To hear the lamentations of poor Anne,

speaker: ANNE
text: Wife to thy Edward, to thy slaughtered son,

speaker: ANNE
text: Stabbed by the selfsame hand that made these wounds.

speaker: ANNE
text: Lo, in these windows that let forth thy life

speaker: ANNE
text: I pour the helpless balm of my poor eyes.

speaker: ANNE
text: O, cursèd be the hand that made these holes;

speaker: ANNE
text: Cursèd the heart that had the heart to do it;

speaker: ANNE
text: Cursèd the blood that let this blood from hence.

speaker: ANNE
text: More direful hap betide that hated wretch

speaker: ANNE
text: That makes us wretched by the death of thee

speaker: ANNE
text: Than I can wish to wolves, to spiders, toads,

speaker: ANNE
text: Or any creeping venomed thing that lives.

speaker: ANNE
text: If ever he have child, abortive be it,

speaker: ANNE
text: Prodigious, and untimely brought to light,

speaker: ANNE
text: Whose ugly and unnatural aspect

speaker: ANNE
text: May fright the hopeful mother at the view,

speaker: ANNE
text: And that be heir to his unhappiness.

speaker: ANNE
text: If ever he have wife, let her be made

speaker: ANNE
text: More miserable by the death of him

speaker: ANNE
text: Than I am made by my young lord and thee.—

speaker: ANNE
text: Come now towards Chertsey with your holy load,

speaker: ANNE
text: Taken from Paul’s to be interrèd there.

speaker: ANNE
text: And still, as you are weary of this weight,

speaker: ANNE
text: Rest you, whiles I lament King Henry’s corse.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Stay, you that bear the corse, and set it down.

speaker: ANNE
text: What black magician conjures up this fiend

speaker: ANNE
text: To stop devoted charitable deeds?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Villains, set down the corse or, by Saint Paul,

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll make a corse of him that disobeys.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: My lord, stand back and let the coffin pass.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Unmannered dog, stand thou when I command!—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Advance thy halberd higher than my breast,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Or by Saint Paul I’ll strike thee to my foot

speaker: RICHARD
text: And spurn upon thee, beggar, for thy boldness.

speaker: ANNE
text: What, do you tremble? Are you all afraid?

speaker: ANNE
text: Alas, I blame you not, for you are mortal,

speaker: ANNE
text: And mortal eyes cannot endure the devil.—

speaker: ANNE
text: Avaunt, thou dreadful minister of hell.

speaker: ANNE
text: Thou hadst but power over his mortal body;

speaker: ANNE
text: His soul thou canst not have. Therefore begone.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Sweet saint, for charity, be not so curst.

speaker: ANNE
text: Foul devil, for God’s sake, hence, and trouble us not,

speaker: ANNE
text: For thou hast made the happy Earth thy hell,

speaker: ANNE
text: Filled it with cursing cries and deep exclaims.

speaker: ANNE
text: If thou delight to view thy heinous deeds,

speaker: ANNE
text: Behold this pattern of thy butcheries.

speaker: ANNE
text: O, gentlemen, see, see dead Henry’s wounds

speaker: ANNE
text: Open their congealed mouths and bleed afresh!—

speaker: ANNE
text: Blush, blush, thou lump of foul deformity,

speaker: ANNE
text: For ’tis thy presence that exhales this blood

speaker: ANNE
text: From cold and empty veins where no blood dwells.

speaker: ANNE
text: Thy deeds, inhuman and unnatural,

speaker: ANNE
text: Provokes this deluge most unnatural.—

speaker: ANNE
text: O God, which this blood mad’st, revenge his death!

speaker: ANNE
text: O Earth, which this blood drink’st, revenge his death!

speaker: ANNE
text: Either heaven with lightning strike the murderer dead,

speaker: ANNE
text: Or Earth gape open wide and eat him quick,

speaker: ANNE
text: As thou dost swallow up this good king’s blood,

speaker: ANNE
text: Which his hell-governed arm hath butcherèd.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Lady, you know no rules of charity,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Which renders good for bad, blessings for curses.

speaker: ANNE
text: Villain, thou know’st nor law of God nor man.

speaker: ANNE
text: No beast so fierce but knows some touch of pity.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But I know none, and therefore am no beast.

speaker: ANNE
text: O, wonderful, when devils tell the truth!

speaker: RICHARD
text: More wonderful, when angels are so angry.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Vouchsafe, divine perfection of a woman,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Of these supposèd crimes to give me leave

speaker: RICHARD
text: By circumstance but to acquit myself.

speaker: ANNE
text: Vouchsafe, defused infection of a man,

speaker: ANNE
text: Of these known evils but to give me leave

speaker: ANNE
text: By circumstance to curse thy cursèd self.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Fairer than tongue can name thee, let me have

speaker: RICHARD
text: Some patient leisure to excuse myself.

speaker: ANNE
text: Fouler than heart can think thee, thou canst make

speaker: ANNE
text: No excuse current but to hang thyself.

speaker: RICHARD
text: By such despair I should accuse myself.

speaker: ANNE
text: And by despairing shalt thou stand excused

speaker: ANNE
text: For doing worthy vengeance on thyself

speaker: ANNE
text: That didst unworthy slaughter upon others.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Say that I slew them not.

speaker: ANNE
text: Then say they were not slain.

speaker: ANNE
text: But dead they are, and, devilish slave, by thee.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I did not kill your husband.

speaker: ANNE
text: Why then, he is alive.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nay, he is dead, and slain by Edward’s hands.

speaker: ANNE
text: In thy foul throat thou liest. Queen Margaret saw

speaker: ANNE
text: Thy murd’rous falchion smoking in his blood,

speaker: ANNE
text: The which thou once didst bend against her breast,

speaker: ANNE
text: But that thy brothers beat aside the point.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I was provokèd by her sland’rous tongue,

speaker: RICHARD
text: That laid their guilt upon my guiltless shoulders.

speaker: ANNE
text: Thou wast provokèd by thy bloody mind,

speaker: ANNE
text: That never dream’st on aught but butcheries.

speaker: ANNE
text: Didst thou not kill this king?

speaker: RICHARD
text: I grant you.

speaker: ANNE
text: Dost grant me, hedgehog? Then, God grant me too

speaker: ANNE
text: Thou mayst be damnèd for that wicked deed.

speaker: ANNE
text: O, he was gentle, mild, and virtuous.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The better for the King of heaven that hath him.

speaker: ANNE
text: He is in heaven, where thou shalt never come.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Let him thank me, that holp to send him thither,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For he was fitter for that place than Earth.

speaker: ANNE
text: And thou unfit for any place but hell.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Yes, one place else, if you will hear me name it.

speaker: ANNE
text: Some dungeon.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Your bedchamber.

speaker: ANNE
text: Ill rest betide the chamber where thou liest!

speaker: RICHARD
text: So will it, madam, till I lie with you.

speaker: ANNE
text: I hope so.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I know so. But, gentle Lady Anne,

speaker: RICHARD
text: To leave this keen encounter of our wits

speaker: RICHARD
text: And fall something into a slower method:

speaker: RICHARD
text: Is not the causer of the timeless deaths

speaker: RICHARD
text: Of these Plantagenets, Henry and Edward,

speaker: RICHARD
text: As blameful as the executioner?

speaker: ANNE
text: Thou wast the cause and most accursed effect.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Your beauty was the cause of that effect—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Your beauty, that did haunt me in my sleep

speaker: RICHARD
text: To undertake the death of all the world,

speaker: RICHARD
text: So I might live one hour in your sweet bosom.

speaker: ANNE
text: If I thought that, I tell thee, homicide,

speaker: ANNE
text: These nails should rend that beauty from my cheeks.

speaker: RICHARD
text: These eyes could not endure that beauty’s wrack.

speaker: RICHARD
text: You should not blemish it, if I stood by.

speaker: RICHARD
text: As all the world is cheerèd by the sun,

speaker: RICHARD
text: So I by that. It is my day, my life.

speaker: ANNE
text: Black night o’ershade thy day, and death thy life.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Curse not thyself, fair creature; thou art both.

speaker: ANNE
text: I would I were, to be revenged on thee.

speaker: RICHARD
text: It is a quarrel most unnatural

speaker: RICHARD
text: To be revenged on him that loveth thee.

speaker: ANNE
text: It is a quarrel just and reasonable

speaker: ANNE
text: To be revenged on him that killed my husband.

speaker: RICHARD
text: He that bereft thee, lady, of thy husband

speaker: RICHARD
text: Did it to help thee to a better husband.

speaker: ANNE
text: His better doth not breathe upon the earth.

speaker: RICHARD
text: He lives that loves thee better than he could.

speaker: ANNE
text: Name him.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Plantagenet.

speaker: ANNE
text: Why, that was he.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The selfsame name, but one of better nature.

speaker: ANNE
text: Where is he?

speaker: RICHARD
text:
Here.
                 
                (
                She
                 
                spits
                 
                at
                 
                him
                .
                )
               Why dost thou spit at me?

speaker: ANNE
text: Would it were mortal poison for thy sake.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Never came poison from so sweet a place.

speaker: ANNE
text: Never hung poison on a fouler toad.

speaker: ANNE
text: Out of my sight! Thou dost infect mine eyes.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thine eyes, sweet lady, have infected mine.

speaker: ANNE
text: Would they were basilisks’ to strike thee dead.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I would they were, that I might die at once,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For now they kill me with a living death.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Those eyes of thine from mine have drawn salt tears,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Shamed their aspects with store of childish drops.

speaker: RICHARD
text: These eyes, which never shed remorseful tear—

speaker: RICHARD
text: No, when my father York and Edward wept

speaker: RICHARD
text: To hear the piteous moan that Rutland made

speaker: RICHARD
text: When black-faced Clifford shook his sword at him;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nor when thy warlike father, like a child,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Told the sad story of my father’s death

speaker: RICHARD
text: And twenty times made pause to sob and weep,

speaker: RICHARD
text: That all the standers-by had wet their cheeks

speaker: RICHARD
text: Like trees bedashed with rain—in that sad time,

speaker: RICHARD
text: My manly eyes did scorn an humble tear;

speaker: RICHARD
text: And what these sorrows could not thence exhale

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thy beauty hath, and made them blind with weeping.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I never sued to friend nor enemy;

speaker: RICHARD
text: My tongue could never learn sweet smoothing word.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But now thy beauty is proposed my fee,

speaker: RICHARD
text: My proud heart sues and prompts my tongue to speak.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Teach not thy lip such scorn, for it was made

speaker: RICHARD
text: For kissing, lady, not for such contempt.

speaker: RICHARD
text: If thy revengeful heart cannot forgive,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Lo, here I lend thee this sharp-pointed sword,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Which if thou please to hide in this true breast

speaker: RICHARD
text: And let the soul forth that adoreth thee,

speaker: RICHARD
text: I lay it naked to the deadly stroke

speaker: RICHARD
text: And humbly beg the death upon my knee.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nay, do not pause, for I did kill King Henry—

speaker: RICHARD
text: But ’twas thy beauty that provokèd me.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nay, now dispatch; ’twas I that stabbed young Edward—

speaker: RICHARD
text: But ’twas thy heavenly face that set me on.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Take up the sword again, or take up me.

speaker: ANNE
text: Arise, dissembler. Though I wish thy death,

speaker: ANNE
text: I will not be thy executioner.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then bid me kill myself, and I will do it.

speaker: ANNE
text: I have already.

speaker: RICHARD
text: That was in thy rage.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Speak it again and, even with the word,

speaker: RICHARD
text: This hand, which for thy love did kill thy love,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Shall for thy love kill a far truer love.

speaker: RICHARD
text: To both their deaths shalt thou be accessory.

speaker: ANNE
text: I would I knew thy heart.

speaker: RICHARD
text: ’Tis figured in my tongue.

speaker: ANNE
text: I fear me both are false.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then never was man true.

speaker: ANNE
text: Well, well, put up your sword.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Say then my peace is made.

speaker: ANNE
text: That shalt thou know hereafter.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But shall I live in hope?

speaker: ANNE
text: All men I hope live so.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Vouchsafe to wear this ring.

speaker: ANNE
text: To take is not to give.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Look how my ring encompasseth thy finger;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Even so thy breast encloseth my poor heart.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Wear both of them, for both of them are thine.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And if thy poor devoted servant may

speaker: RICHARD
text: But beg one favor at thy gracious hand,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thou dost confirm his happiness forever.

speaker: ANNE
text: What is it?

speaker: RICHARD
text: That it may please you leave these sad designs

speaker: RICHARD
text: To him that hath most cause to be a mourner,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And presently repair to Crosby House,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Where, after I have solemnly interred

speaker: RICHARD
text: At Chertsey monast’ry this noble king

speaker: RICHARD
text: And wet his grave with my repentant tears,

speaker: RICHARD
text: I will with all expedient duty see you.

speaker: RICHARD
text: For divers unknown reasons, I beseech you,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Grant me this boon.

speaker: ANNE
text: With all my heart, and much it joys me too

speaker: ANNE
text: To see you are become so penitent.—

speaker: ANNE
text: Tressel and Berkeley, go along with me.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Bid me farewell.

speaker: ANNE
text: ’Tis more than you deserve;

speaker: ANNE
text: But since you teach me how to flatter you,

speaker: ANNE
text: Imagine I have said “farewell” already.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Towards Chertsey, noble lord?

speaker: RICHARD
text: No, to Whitefriars. There attend my coming.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Was ever woman in this humor wooed?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Was ever woman in this humor won?

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll have her, but I will not keep her long.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What, I that killed her husband and his father,

speaker: RICHARD
text: To take her in her heart’s extremest hate,

speaker: RICHARD
text: With curses in her mouth, tears in her eyes,

speaker: RICHARD
text: The bleeding witness of my hatred by,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Having God, her conscience, and these bars against me,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And I no friends to back my suit at all

speaker: RICHARD
text: But the plain devil and dissembling looks?

speaker: RICHARD
text: And yet to win her, all the world to nothing!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ha!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Hath she forgot already that brave prince,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Edward, her lord, whom I some three months since

speaker: RICHARD
text: Stabbed in my angry mood at Tewkesbury?

speaker: RICHARD
text: A sweeter and a lovelier gentleman,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Framed in the prodigality of nature,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Young, valiant, wise, and, no doubt, right royal,

speaker: RICHARD
text: The spacious world cannot again afford.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And will she yet abase her eyes on me,

speaker: RICHARD
text: That cropped the golden prime of this sweet prince

speaker: RICHARD
text: And made her widow to a woeful bed?

speaker: RICHARD
text: On me, whose all not equals Edward’s moiety?

speaker: RICHARD
text: On me, that halts and am misshapen thus?

speaker: RICHARD
text: My dukedom to a beggarly denier,

speaker: RICHARD
text: I do mistake my person all this while!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Upon my life, she finds, although I cannot,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Myself to be a marv’lous proper man.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll be at charges for a looking glass

speaker: RICHARD
text: And entertain a score or two of tailors

speaker: RICHARD
text: To study fashions to adorn my body.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Since I am crept in favor with myself,

speaker: RICHARD
text: I will maintain it with some little cost.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But first I’ll turn yon fellow in his grave

speaker: RICHARD
text: And then return lamenting to my love.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Shine out, fair sun, till I have bought a glass,

speaker: RICHARD
text: That I may see my shadow as I pass.
